summary: REQ-1 appears implemented via a footer technology-stack notice in the React App. However, several additional, unrequested changes were made (branding/landing page overhaul, Tailwind/theme changes, SEO/title change, and project metadata claiming an HTML/JS + Django stack), which were not part of the BuildRequest and in one case contradict the stated constraints.

hallucinated_features.description: Reworked the app into a full UNICO single-page landing page (header/about/services/products/banner/contact sections), beyond adding the requested footer notice.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest only asked for a clear, user-facing notice in the UI footer; it did not request a full landing-page redesign or additional sections/behaviors.

hallucinated_features.description: Added smooth-scroll behavior by attaching click handlers to all anchor links.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested in REQ-1; unrelated to adding a footer notice about the stack.

hallucinated_features.description: Introduced/modified global brand styling and Tailwind design tokens (navy/gold, OKLCH tokens, custom utilities).
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested in REQ-1; BuildRequest only required a footer notice and avoiding Python/Django references in the UI.

hallucinated_features.description: Changed HTML entry point metadata/title (document title set to "I" and SEO metadata described as "complete").
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested in REQ-1; BuildRequest did not ask for SEO or title changes.

hallucinated_features.description: Updated project metadata to state the project uses an HTML/CSS/JavaScript frontend and a Python/Django backend.
hallucinated_features.reason: Contradicts the BuildRequest constraints (stack is fixed to React+TypeScript frontend and Motoko backend) and was not requested as part of REQ-1 (which explicitly treats such a migration as non-actionable).
